relationTypes.supports: The source or fact directly supports a claim or thesis.
relationTypes.supports_with_scope: Supports the frame only within a scoped or hedged wording.
relationTypes.supports_but_limits: Supports the direction while limiting overclaim.
relationTypes.supports_counterargument: Supports a counterargument or reviewer-proofing point.
relationTypes.challenges: Contradicts or materially weakens a claim.
relationTypes.challenges_overclaim: Does not refute the whole fact, but blocks a stronger wording.
relationTypes.walks_back: A rollback, clarification or public/analytical walkback.
relationTypes.updates: A newer fact changes the status or mechanism of an earlier one.
relationTypes.sets_up: Creates the premise for the next event or layer.
relationTypes.escalates: Moves control or risk higher up the stack.
relationTypes.routes_around: A workaround or alternative route through the stack.
relationTypes.mitigates: Reduces the strength of a risk or bottleneck.
relationTypes.limits: Adds a gap or caveat that requires hedging.
relationTypes.refines: Reframes a claim into a more precise wording.
relationTypes.parallel: Shows an analogous pattern in another domain.
relationTypes.generalizes: Transfers a mechanism from one domain to another.
relationTypes.institutionalizes: Shows a shift from pilot/event to durable institution.
relationTypes.supports_arc: Connects a claim directly to a story arc.
relationTypes.supports_mechanism: Supports the mechanism behind the arc.
relationTypes.supports_as_claim_not_fact: Supports the fact that a claim was made, not the claim as established fact.
relationTypes.supports_with_caveat: Supports the point only with an explicit caveat.
relationTypes.triggers_capital_and_cloud_lockin: Triggers capital and cloud lock-in dynamics.
relationTypes.produces_workaround: Produces a workaround that later becomes part of the control cycle.
relationTypes.closed_by: Closed or constrained by a later fact.

edgeVisualHints.rank_suggestion: Use manual edges first for visual storytelling; hide AUTO_* edges for simplified slides.

gaps.topic: Exact Stanford AI Index 2026 investment numbers
gaps.why: Slides need hard country and sector comparisons.
gaps.what_would_close: Extract official AI Index 2026 tables for private AI investment, corporate investment, model counts, compute and sovereignty framework.
gaps.resolution: Primary AI Index 2026 figures captured (SIG_2026_STANFORD_*): $285.9B/$12.4B private, $344.7B global private (+127.5%), $581.7B corporate (+130%), 50 vs 30 models, 5,427 DCs, -89% talent, 2.7% model gap. Guidance-fund clarification: $184B AI vs $912B all-industry. Residual: pull exact PDF table cells for footnotes.
gaps.title: Exact Stanford AI Index 2026 investment numbers

gaps.topic: Pledge vs committed vs allocated vs spent vs deployed
gaps.why: Most sovereign AI claims are vulnerable to mixing incomparable money categories.
gaps.what_would_close: Add money_status and deployment_status to every investment record.
gaps.title: Pledge vs committed vs allocated vs spent vs deployed

gaps.topic: Current post-AI-Diffusion-rule legal architecture
gaps.why: The January 2025 tier map cannot be treated as current without caveats.
gaps.what_would_close: BIS/Federal Register memo tracking what survived, was rescinded, or moved into guidance.
gaps.title: Current post-AI-Diffusion-rule legal architecture

gaps.topic: H20/H200/Blackwell policy status and licenses
gaps.why: Core to US-China export-control storyline.
gaps.what_would_close: Nvidia filings + BIS guidance + Reuters/AP timeline rows.
gaps.resolution: Current status captured (SIG_2026_BIS_H200_*, SIG_2026_BLACKWELL_*): H200/MI325X case-by-case (25% tariff, 50% cap, KYC, inspection), Blackwell presumption-of-denial, H20 re-permitted under 15% arrangement, Huawei 600k Ascend 910C 2026, Beijing ambivalent. Residual: confirm Beijing import approval and Huawei actual output.
gaps.title: H20/H200/Blackwell policy status and licenses

gaps.topic: Confirmed government AI decision-support deployments
gaps.why: Needed for the 'institutional mind / knowledge structure capture' section.
gaps.what_would_close: Procurement and policy records for defense, intelligence, finance, procurement and critical infrastructure.
gaps.title: Confirmed government AI decision-support deployments

gaps.topic: Real-world AI-accelerated offensive cyber operations
gaps.why: Machine-speed offense is the most review-vulnerable claim.
gaps.what_would_close: Independent incident reports with IoCs or credible third-party validation; avoid operational exploit details.
gaps.title: Real-world AI-accelerated offensive cyber operations

gaps.topic: Whether open weights transfer sovereignty or only model artifacts
gaps.why: Core counterargument to stack-dependence thesis.
gaps.what_would_close: Compare DeepSeek/Qwen/Kimi/Huawei Ascend/SMIC stack with cloud, chips, training curves, inference cost and deployment capacity.
gaps.title: Whether open weights transfer sovereignty or only model artifacts

gaps.topic: Russia's actual frontier compute and cloud access
gaps.why: Country card is currently stronger on governance/autarky than compute measurement.
gaps.what_would_close: Evidence on Nvidia/CUDA access, China alternatives, domestic accelerators, datacenters and model training.
gaps.title: Russia's actual frontier compute and cloud access

gaps.topic: Primary U.S. order/license text for Mythos/Fable restrictions
gaps.why: Needed to determine whether this is legally export control, licensing guidance, voluntary pre-release review, or ad hoc access control.
gaps.what_would_close: Commerce/BIS order, letter, Federal Register notice, or released license terms.
gaps.resolution: Legal basis identified (SIG_2026_BIS_ANTHROPIC_ISINFORMED_LETTER): BIS 'is-informed' letter, Lutnick to Amodei, 12 Jun 2026; ECRA 50 USC 4817(b)(1) + EAR 744.22(b); Bloomberg has a copy. Residual: government still has not published the official order text; legal validity contested and in litigation.
gaps.title: Primary U.S. order/license text for Mythos/Fable restrictions

gaps.topic: Public technical details of Mythos cyber evaluation
gaps.why: Prevents overclaiming from press accounts and partner anecdotes.
gaps.what_would_close: AISI/NSA/Anthropic benchmark descriptions, target classes, success criteria, false positive rate, triage workload.
gaps.title: Public technical details of Mythos cyber evaluation

gaps.topic: Regulator-confirmed 105 vulnerabilities claimed by 360
gaps.why: Key number in China counter-stack narrative is a vendor claim.
gaps.what_would_close: CNVD/CNNVD entries, regulator statement, CVE/advisory mapping, Microsoft acknowledgements.
gaps.title: Regulator-confirmed 105 vulnerabilities claimed by 360

gaps.topic: Project Glasswing membership, country count and token credits
gaps.why: Habr/Zhou speech gives 15 countries, >200 orgs and up to $100M token credits, but this is unverified.
gaps.what_would_close: Official Glasswing participant list, access tiers and credit terms.
gaps.title: Project Glasswing membership, country count and token credits

gaps.topic: Primary contracts and procurement documents for Pentagon/OpenAI/Google/Palantir deployments
gaps.why: Critical-use claims depend heavily on Reuters/The Information reporting and classified agreements.
gaps.what_would_close: DoD announcements, contract excerpts, procurement numbers, model-use restrictions, audit/logging terms.
gaps.resolution: Primary obtained for Palantir Army EA (W519TC-25-D-0039) and Anthropic $200M DoD agreement (cogsec-03). Residual: task-order-level primary docs for OpenAI/Google/xAI and Palantir Maven/TITAN/ICE task orders.
gaps.title: Primary contracts and procurement documents for Pentagon/OpenAI/Google/Palantir deployments

gaps.topic: Concrete examples of Anthropic/OpenAI vetted exemptions for dual-use bio/science users
gaps.why: Anthropic confirms the access-control mechanism but not who gets exemptions or how decisions are made.
gaps.what_would_close: Documented exemption process, institutional affiliation requirements, approved categories, appeal process.
gaps.title: Concrete examples of Anthropic/OpenAI vetted exemptions for dual-use bio/science users

gaps.topic: Whether banks have actual AI kill switches, inventories and independent validations
gaps.why: Regulators ask for controls, but deployment maturity is unclear.
gaps.what_would_close: Bank model-risk disclosures, examination findings, incident reports, third-party audit evidence.
gaps.resolution: RBI draft AI/ML framework (kill-switch/decommissioning, independent validation, board accountability) + US bank-regulator scrutiny + UK finance AI stress tests captured (CLM_025). Residual: final circulars, supervisory findings, and proof of actual decommissioning implementations (see GAP_023).
gaps.title: Whether banks have actual AI kill switches, inventories and independent validations

gaps.topic: Primary or independently verified evidence for Lavender/Gospel targeting workflow
gaps.why: Guardian/+972 evidence is important but contested and based on anonymous sources.
gaps.what_would_close: Official documents, independent inquiry, declassified procedure, legal assessment.
gaps.title: Primary or independently verified evidence for Lavender/Gospel targeting workflow

gaps.topic: Primary Palantir contracts and task orders
gaps.why: Several key claims rely on press reports and contract ceilings.
gaps.what_would_close: DoD, Army, NATO, UK MoD, NHS, ICE contract documents with scope, data-rights, access, portability and audit terms.
gaps.resolution: Primary record captured (SIG_2025_PALANTIR_ARMY_10B_PRIMARY): army.mil release + federal award W519TC-25-D-0039, 10-yr IDC, $10B ceiling, $0 obligated at award, 75 contracts consolidated. Money-discipline correction logged (ceiling != spend).
gaps.title: Primary Palantir contracts and task orders

gaps.topic: Independent audits of Palantir decision impact
gaps.why: Need to separate workflow adoption from proven operational benefit.
gaps.what_would_close: Independent audits of Maven targeting accuracy, NHS FDP outcomes, ICE enforcement outcomes, Met Police false positives.
gaps.title: Independent audits of Palantir decision impact

gaps.topic: Legal ownership vs practical operational control of data
gaps.why: The strongest counterargument is that Palantir is a processor/vendor under client control.
gaps.what_would_close: Contractual analysis of data ownership, admin access, keys, portability, logging, staff access and subcontractors.
gaps.title: Legal ownership vs practical operational control of data

gaps.topic: Scale of AI-chip smuggling and transshipment via Malaysia/Singapore/UAE
gaps.why: A single seizure proves attempts and enforcement, not total leakage volume.
gaps.what_would_close: Customs case records, U.S./Malaysia enforcement data, chip model identification, destination country and prosecution outcome.
gaps.resolution: Documented via three DOJ cases (export-18 / SIG_2026_DOJ_SUPERMICRO_2_5B_SMUGGLING, SIG_2025_DOJ_ALX_SOLUTIONS_TRANSSHIPMENT, SIG_2025_DOJ_JANFORD_GPU_SMUGGLING) + Taiwan first prosecution. Case values: ~$2.5B scheme ($510M in weeks), 400 A100, 20+ SG/MY shipments. Residual: total gray-market leakage volume remains unquantified.
gaps.title: Scale of AI-chip smuggling and transshipment via Malaysia/Singapore/UAE

gaps.topic: Hard project-level data on delayed/cancelled AI data-center projects
gaps.why: Backlash and grid stress are critical counterforces, but project-delay totals are often advocacy/aggregator figures.
gaps.what_would_close: Project-level list with status, capacity, value, developer, jurisdiction, reason for delay and primary local records.
gaps.resolution: Hard data captured (SIG_2026_DATACENTER_CANCELLATIONS_HARD_DATA): Sightline/Bloomberg ~5 of 16 GW under construction (30-50% delayed/cancelled); Data Center Watch 75+ projects / $130B blocked Q1; Heatmap 20 cancelled / $41.7B / 3.5GW Q1; Baird 2->6->25 cancellations. Residual: standardized project-level registry with primary local records.
gaps.title: Hard project-level data on delayed/cancelled AI data-center projects

gaps.topic: Primary evidence for Ukrainian/Russian autonomous targeting and drone-model data pipelines
gaps.why: War data flywheel is central but much current evidence is company/media reporting.
gaps.what_would_close: Government procurement, company release, model card, testing results, or fielded-system documentation without operational attack details.
gaps.title: Primary evidence for Ukrainian/Russian autonomous targeting and drone-model data pipelines

gaps.topic: Final form of RBI and other financial AI model-risk rules
gaps.why: Draft rules show direction, but final obligations and enforcement remain open.
gaps.what_would_close: Final circulars, supervisory examination findings, bank model inventories and evidence of kill-switch/decommissioning implementations.
gaps.title: Final form of RBI and other financial AI model-risk rules

gaps.topic: Public legitimacy thresholds for military AI by country and scenario
gaps.why: Military AI adoption may be more politically feasible than expected, but autonomy red lines differ.
gaps.what_would_close: Peer-reviewed cross-national survey, longitudinal polling and country-specific elite/policy responses.
gaps.title: Public legitimacy thresholds for military AI by country and scenario

palantirCase.position_in_thesis: Palantir is a practical example of structural-power compression at the decision-support layer: security + knowledge + production + finance.
core_mechanism: operational ontology and common operating picture
permissioning, audit trails and human-in-the-loop interfaces
strongest_supporting_facts: NHS FDP £330M contract and access controversy
strongest_counterarguments: Palantir often acts as processor/vendor rather than legal data owner
critical performance claims may lack causal proof
many contracts are ceilings or frameworks, not spent money
some military targeting claims rely on classified or anonymous-source reporting
public-sector resistance and procurement scrutiny can interrupt expansion

contested_or_needs_more_independent_vali.description: Anthropic/Qihoo/vendor claims about AI-assisted or AI-orchestrated cyber operations should be coded as disputed unless accompanied by independent IoCs, technical reports or third-party validation.

threat_map.threat: Indirect prompt injection in web/RAG inputs
threat_map.mechanism: Hidden machine-facing instructions embedded in webpages or HTTP responses steer model output or agent actions.
threat_map.gap: Need production incidents in government/enterprise RAG.

threat_map.threat: Prompt injection in hiring / resume screening
threat_map.mechanism: Applicants embed hidden instructions in resumes consumed by LLM-based screening workflows.
threat_map.gap: Need more platforms and non-hiring workflows.

threat_map.threat: Unsafe agentic deployment with weak transparency
threat_map.mechanism: Deployed agents act across tools while public safety/evaluation transparency remains uneven.
threat_map.gap: Need deployment registry for government and critical infrastructure agents.

new_or_updated_claims_recommended: Cloud lock-in is measurable but legally and contractually contestable.
Material influence through compute and capital is not the same as de facto control.
Decision sovereignty is a data-rights, architecture and portability problem.
Open weights provide a delayed model-layer exit, not full-stack reproducibility.
War data is an access-controlled training pipeline, not proof of autonomous targeting.
AI cyber capability is rising rapidly while realistic multi-stage autonomy remains unreliable.
Provider telemetry shows malicious AI use moving deeper into the attack lifecycle, but banned accounts are not successful breaches.
AI workflow platforms are a repeatedly exploited credential-rich attack surface even when the attacker is a conventional script rather than an LLM agent.
JadePuffer is a strong operational agentic-offense signal, but remains single-vendor and attribution-limited.
Mycelium is an underground product-design signal, not evidence of an operating AI botnet.
Authorization claims are now an attack input: recovered OALABS logs show a weak operator steering agents through real intrusions by presenting criminal work as an approved red-team exercise.

Published agent skills form an executable instruction supply chain; confirmed malicious artifacts include credential thieves and agent-hijacking instructions, but repository counts are not victim counts.

A trusted assistant can be both the persuaded deputy and the persuasive intermediary: controlled tests moved malicious instructions through the user into the agent and back through the agent into the user.
Agentjacking demonstrates authority spoofing through trusted telemetry in controlled real-world environments; Friendly Fire and HalluSquatting remain reproducible PoCs rather than wild campaigns.

Selective Permeability supplies reproducible self-authored mechanism evidence: explicit user-context policy polarity can dominate a token's functional referent, while token output and compaction can break bearer-style provenance.
RAND measures a sharp public-model skill-floor shift on bounded CTFs while preserving the shared-target, human-intervention and no-active-defender caveats.
Energy is locally binding and globally bounded.
Sovereign flow gating now includes concrete Chinese deal unwind, European outbound monitoring and reciprocal corporate software-access controls.
arc_update_recommended: Expand ARC_CLOUD_CAPACITY_VENDOR_LOCKIN into a paired lock-in/remedy arc.
Add independent GAO audit nodes to ARC_PALANTIR_DECISION_OS without naming an anonymous contractor.
Replace secondary Ukraine footage wording with the official governed-platform fact.
Add EchoLeak as production surface evidence and preserve the no-known-exploitation caveat.
Add the Anthropic 832-account corpus and the four-entry Langflow KEV cluster to ARC_CYBER_CLAIM_TO_CAVEAT; expose the provider-telemetry and no-LLM counterexamples.
Add JadePuffer to ARC_CYBER_CLAIM_TO_CAVEAT as D-level operational evidence with the human-setup and attribution caveats visible.
Add Mycelium to ARC_CYBER_CLAIM_TO_CAVEAT only as a P1 advertised-architecture node with no proof-of-execution.
Add OALABS and RAND to ARC_CYBER_CLAIM_TO_CAVEAT as respectively real-world misuse and controlled skill-floor evidence, keeping human direction and CTF limitations visible.

Expand ARC_COGSEC_LAB_TO_WILD_TO_STATE with a provenance ladder: malicious skills and OALABS in the wild; Anthropic/Pentera and Agentjacking as controlled validation; Friendly Fire and HalluSquatting as PoCs.
Place Selective Permeability at the mechanism end of the provenance ladder and label it self-authored synthetic evidence rather than external validation.

Render trusted-role cues explicitly: false authorization, forged telemetry, defensive documentation, synced assistant preferences and hallucinated resource names are distinct manipulation channels, not one generic prompt-injection label.
Add the Alibaba Claude Code ban to ARC_QUIET_ACCESS_CONTROL and ARC_SOVEREIGN_FLOW_GATING, while separating the corporate ban from the MIIT-affiliated warning and any nationwide legal restriction.
Add Manus NDRC order to ARC_SOVEREIGN_FLOW_GATING; keep travel restrictions in a hold state.

hold_or_rejected.claim: Chinese authorities barred Manus co-founders from leaving the country during the Meta review.
Tencent-led investors will buy Manus back at the original reported valuation.
Manus annualized revenue rose to $400–500M.
BIS rescinded the AI Diffusion Rule on 13 May 2025.
EchoLeak was exploited in a real customer breach.
A downloadable weight file makes a model fully open source and sovereign.
AI third-party concentration is a financial-stability vulnerability.
Anthropic reported that an AI agent automated reconnaissance, credential theft and attacks on corporate networks.
Researchers discovered an operating Mycelium AI botnet that automates the full attack lifecycle.
Claude Code secretly uploaded Chinese developers' repositories or source files to Anthropic.
HalluSquatting has already created a mass botnet or DDoS network through Claude Code and other agents.
The real geopy package was poisoned and compromised Claude Code and Codex users in the wild.
Attackers compromised 2,388 organizations through Agentjacking.
Sysdig observed LLM-driven post-exploitation through marimo in May 2026.
LLM agents began exploiting CVE-2026-39987 within ten hours of disclosure.
TeamPCP was an AI-agent campaign because it compromised LiteLLM and other developer tools.
An autonomous agent poisoned McKinsey Lilli's system prompts and manipulated consultants.
Incalmo fully and autonomously compromised nine real 25-50-host networks.
The 48%, 82% and 36% figures independently prove an agentic attack wave.
More than 3,000 organizations were compromised by malicious AI skills.
OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026 is evidence of a specific attack campaign.

chronologyAudit.date_basis_rule: Use the date of the concrete public, legal, commercial, or enforcement milestone. Do not backdate a later availability event to an earlier development or disclosure date.

audit_findings.finding_en: GPT-2 cannot be dated to 2015. OpenAI announced the staged-release experiment on 14 February 2019 and released the 1.5B model with a model card on 5 November 2019.
audit_findings.current_v015_state: No GPT-2 evidence card is present in the published v0.15 package. The reported 2015 card belongs to another or intermediate timeline.
audit_findings.correction: Add a 2019-11-05 event and preserve 2019-02-14 as the start of the staged-release sequence in its notes.

audit_findings.finding_en: Google publicly disclosed its internally deployed TPU on 18 May 2016. Metered Cloud TPU access began in public beta on 12 February 2018 at $6.50 per TPU-hour, billed by the second.
audit_findings.current_v015_state: No Cloud TPU evidence card is present in the published v0.15 package. The reported 2016 hourly-access card belongs to another or intermediate timeline.
audit_findings.correction: Represent disclosure and commercial availability as two separate events in 2016 and 2018.

audit_findings.finding_en: The empty 2021 year is a collection-boundary artifact. The previous formation-phase backfill started in 2022, even though major legal, strategic, export-control and workflow-embedding milestones occurred in 2021.
audit_findings.correction: Add ten primary-sourced 2021 events and revise the formation claim so that 2022 is an escalation phase, not the origin point.

overclaims_rejected.claim: The FY2021 NDAA funded the later $50 billion CHIPS for America program.
overclaims_rejected.reason: Public Law 116-283 authorized semiconductor incentive, R&D and workforce programs. The CHIPS and Science Act of 2022 supplied the major appropriation.

overclaims_rejected.claim: The 2021 EU AI Act proposal was already binding law.
overclaims_rejected.reason: COM(2021) 206 was a Commission proposal. Political agreement followed in 2023 and the final regulation entered into force in 2024.

overclaims_rejected.claim: GitHub Copilot's 2021 launch proves copyright infringement or durable developer lock-in.
overclaims_rejected.reason: The launch and use of public source code in the training corpus are documented. Legal infringement and dependency require separate evidence.

overclaims_rejected.claim: The NSCAI report itself imposed defense or export-control obligations.
overclaims_rejected.reason: It was an official commission report and strategic recommendation, not a binding rule.

overclaims_rejected.claim: The FTC complaint was a final adjudication that the Nvidia-Arm transaction was unlawful.
overclaims_rejected.reason: The complaint documented the agency's competition theory. Nvidia abandoned the deal in February 2022 before a final merits decision.

families.summary_en: Export controls, licensing, user vetting, workaround closure and cross-border flow controls.
Alternative routes, open weights and national perimeters that reduce one dependency while creating others.
Machine speed, agentic attacks, prompt injection, decision support and feedback from military use.
Cloud capacity, power systems and distinct money regimes as lower-stack constraints.
Operational interfaces, licensed data, enterprise adoption and governed shutdown.
Timeline phases from proprietary accelerators and metered access to law, doctrine and the governance shock.

factcheckAudit.anti_overfit_rule_en: Do not promote a lab study, vendor scan, public exposure measurement, proposal or forecast into an incident, prevalence estimate, enacted law or measured outcome.
corrected_overclaims: The EU Cybersecurity and AI Action Plan creates the 3% GPAI fine.
CADA is already enacted law.
The 69% and 70% cloud figures use the same denominator and date.
SecNumCloud bans every non-EU ownership stake.
21,639 public OpenClaw instances were vulnerable or compromised.
OpenClaw public exposure and CVE-2026-25253 are one event.
The USAF drone thought experiment or CoastRunners demonstrates structural-power incidents.
Agentic cyber evidence leaves only strategic scale unproven.
Hunt.io independently confirmed Anthropic GTG-1002.
The Hunt.io corpus proves a fully autonomous attack.
Hunt.io attributed the operation to the Chinese state.
Four national governments or 5,890 hosts were breached.
Xi explicitly named the United States or used the phrase closed club in his WAIC speech.
WAICO is already an operational and universally representative global AI regulator.
The 5,000 training-place pledge is delivered technology transfer or unrestricted model access.
Soofi S has completed a general permissive open-source release.
Soofi S is universally 8-9x faster or has demonstrated 8-9x lower energy use.
Training or hosting a model in Europe automatically makes every deployment GDPR-compliant.
South Korea promised a Mythos-equivalent cybersecurity model by the end of 2026.
HyperCLOVA X has been selected as the base for South Korea's cybersecurity model.
Naver's former AI chief still serves as the current senior presidential AI secretary after resigning on 28 April 2026.
Naver was generally banned from Korean sovereign or defense AI after its contest exclusion.
The Anthropic MOU restored Mythos access or transferred frontier-model technology to Korea.
Project Canopy independently verified 990 exploitable CVEs in deployed Korean government systems.
The Naver-KAI MOU proves a deployed defense model or autonomous combat capability.
Hugging Face disclosed the incident on 6 July 2026.
Hugging Face proved the first fully autonomous cyberattack with no human operator.
The Hugging Face attacker used OpenAI, Anthropic, GLM or a named model.
The Hugging Face incident is attributable to JADEPUFFER or a named threat group.
The 17,000 recorded events establish 17,000 autonomous attack decisions.
Public dataset-viewer PRs are an official one-to-one postmortem or exact exploit chain.
Commercial frontier models are generally unusable for cyber defense.
GLM 5.2 or open weights are intrinsically safer or create full-stack sovereignty.
Kimi K3 full weights, technical report and final license were already public on 16-17 July 2026.
Kimi K3 independently proves parity with the leading US frontier models or Chinese full-stack sovereignty.
Dean Ball announced OpenAI or US government policy to spread FUD about Chinese models.
A Federal Reserve bulletin found backdoors in Kimi or another Chinese model.
The wp2shell chain was autonomously targeted and discovered without expert direction.
A novice can reproduce wp2shell with vague prompts.
The complete wp2shell research cost was $25 or a broker offered $500,000 for this exact chain.
Production exploitation of wp2shell proves that attackers used GPT-5.6 or another LLM.

rolling_news_review.review: Three atomic records retained. Kimi is a verified service launch with an open-weight commitment still pending. Ball is retained as a contested public articulation of a possible control mechanism, not policy. wp2shell is retained as a high-quality expert-led discovery outlier with confirmed operational impact and explicit human direction.
